ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RASIER-CA, LLC, RASIER, LLC, UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., UBER USA, LLC, AND UATC, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND THIS CASE IS STAYING PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. THE DELEGATION CLAUSE IN THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT CLEARLY AND UNMISTAKABLY STATES THAT THE ARBITRATOR WILL DECIDE "THE ENFORCEABILITY, REVOCABILITY OR VALIDITY OF THE ARBITRATION PROVISION OR ANY PORTION OF THE ARBITRATION PROVISION." (DECEMBER 2015 TSA, SECTION 15.I.). PLAINTIFF SHAUN SMITH DOES NOT SUBSTANTIVELY CHALLENGE THE DELEGATION CLAUSE. THEREFORE, THE ARBITRATOR MUST DECIDE WHETHER THERE EXISTS AN ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT THAT WILL REQUIRE THE PARTIES TO ARBITRATE THE UNDERLYING MERITS OF THIS LABOR DISPUTE OR WHETHER MR. SMITH OPTED OUT OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. (O'CONNOR V. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. (9TH CIR. 2018) 904 F.3D 1087, 1094-1095). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)
RESPONSE TO ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE (TRANSACTION ID # 66119847) FILED BY DEFENDANT RASIER-CA, LLC RAISER, LLC UBER USA, LLC UATC LLC UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF AUG-10-2022 CONTINUED TO DEC-14-2022 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR STATUS OF ARBITRATION STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 68943786)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gal Gressel, Esq., Littler Mendelson, P.C., Telephone 415-677-3175, appearing for Defendant
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Rasier-CA, LLC, Rasier, LLC, Uber Technologies, Inc., Uber USA, LLC, and UATC, LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is staying pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings. The delegation clause in the parties' agreement clearly and unmistakably states that the arbitrator will decide "the enforceability, revocability or validity of the Arbitration Provision or any portion of the Arbitration Provision." (December 2015 TSA, Section 15.i.). Plaintiff Shaun Smith does not substantively challenge the delegation clause. Therefore, the arbitrator must decide whether there exists an enforceable arbitration agreement that will require the parties to arbitrate the underlying merits of this labor dispute or whether Mr. Smith opted out of the arbitration agreement. (O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (9th Cir. 2018) 904 F.3d 1087, 1094-1095). Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
